_readme: S5.7 offline eval gold set for advisory briefs (briefs:eval). Each case is a representative DCP digest plus a human-graded target brief. expect=pass means a correctly calibrated judge should score the target brief at/above the rubric threshold; expect=fail cases are deliberately weak briefs the judge must reject. Extend this set from real labeled briefs in the admin review screen — 'actually good' labeled briefs make new pass cases, 'actually bad' ones make fail cases.

cases.dcp_digest:
Family-run artisan bakery in a mid-size town, strong local walk-in trade but almost no online presence.
Digital maturity: low
Pain points: no time to post; doesn't know what to photograph; competitors visible on Instagram
Goals: attract younger customers; take pre-orders for custom cakes
Strengths: distinctive sourdough range; loyal regulars; photogenic storefront

target_brief.paragraph: Your sourdough range and custom cakes are the two things nobody nearby can copy, and they photograph well — which matters more than posting often. The pre-order goal changes what your profile is for: it should work like a counter, not a brochure.
bullets: Lead with the cakes: they are the product people plan and search for, so they justify a booking flow.
Your regulars are your unpaid content team — their habit of coming back is the story younger customers respond to.
The storefront is a stage; one good weekly photo beats daily filler you don't have time for.

cases.dcp_digest:
Independent auto-repair shop, two lifts, owner quotes by phone only.
Digital maturity: medium
Pain points: no-shows for booked slots; quoting takes evenings; review count far below rivals
Goals: fill Tuesday-Thursday capacity; stop losing fleet clients to chains
Strengths: honest-pricing reputation; fast turnaround on diagnostics

target_brief.paragraph: The no-show and evening-quoting problems are the same problem: everything routes through your phone. Fleet clients in particular are choosing chains for predictability, not price — your honest-pricing reputation only wins if it is visible where they compare.
bullets: Midweek capacity is a scheduling-visibility issue, not a demand issue — the diagnostics speed is your lever there.
Reviews are the fleet buyer's shortlist filter; the gap to rivals costs you before you ever quote.

cases.dcp_digest:
Малък фризьорски салон в квартал с много конкуренция, силна база от постоянни клиентки.
Digital maturity: low
Pain points: празни часове в понеделник и вторник; клиентките резервират само по телефона
Goals: повече млади клиентки; онлайн резервации
Strengths: колористка с награди; лоялни клиентки от години

target_brief.paragraph: Наградената колористка е това, което никой съседен салон не може да претендира — тя е причината млада клиентка да прекоси квартала. Празните понеделници и телефонните резервации са свързани: без онлайн запазване никой не вижда кога сте свободни.
bullets: Портфолиото на колористката е по-силен аргумент от всякаква промоция.
Лоялните клиентки са мостът към дъщерите и приятелките им — препоръката е вашият канал.

cases.dcp_digest:
Boutique florist, weddings are the high-margin work but walk-ins dominate the schedule.
Digital maturity: low
Pain points: wedding leads arrive too late in the season; website is a single outdated page
Goals: more wedding bookings; look premium online
Strengths: styled-shoot portfolio; venue partnerships

target_brief.paragraph: Social media is a great way to grow your business. By showing up online regularly and connecting with your audience, you can build trust and attract more customers over time.
bullets: Share behind-the-scenes content to seem authentic.
Use relevant hashtags to increase your reach.
Stay active and respond to comments.

cases.dcp_digest:
Personal-training studio, three trainers, classes sell out but 1:1 slots do not.
Digital maturity: medium
Pain points: 1:1 slots unsold; Instagram followers don't convert
Goals: fill 1:1 slots; launch an online programme
Strengths: strong class community; before/after results archive

target_brief.paragraph: Here is your content plan for next week. Monday: post 'Struggling to stay consistent? Our 1:1 coaching is for you — link in bio!'. Wednesday: share a before/after with the caption 'Real results from real people'. Friday: run a story poll asking 'Classes or 1:1?'.
bullets: Monday caption: 'New week, new goals 💪 Book your free consult today!'
